brief: Windows CI produces 0 findings in backward trace analysis. Mac CI produces correct results with the same commit.

root_cause.description: fqn.split(':')[0] returns the Windows drive letter ('D') instead of the file path on absolute paths
root_cause.mechanism: colon collision between Windows drive letter separator and FQN separator

required_findings.description: Agent must identify the backward-trace module/subsystem
Agent must identify the colon-split as the failure mechanism
Agent must explain the Windows-specific colon collision
Agent must identify backward-trace.ts around line 43 as the exact location

anti_patterns.description: Blaming git (the version control system) as the root cause rather than the parsing code is incorrect
This is a path separator issue, not a character encoding issue
Root cause is in application code, not CI configuration

notes: The safe pattern using lastIndexOf(':') already exists at line 105 (findLogNode). A perfect investigation will also note this.
